video:video.video:title: ASEAN leaders meet for summit under pressure to mitigate backlash from war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Southeast Asian leaders expressed "serious concern" over the war in Iran and called for a "cessation of hostilities" as they met in Philippines on Friday for an annual summit.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano eruption kills at least 3 hikers, 17 injured
video:video.video:description: Mount Dukono erupted in Indonesia’s North Maluku province, killing three hikers, including two Singaporean nationals and one Indonesian, authorities said Friday, as the volcano continued to spew ash high into the air and prompted renewed safety warnings.

video:video.video:title: Dozens of artists bring new life to gigantic former ironworks on UNESCO's world heritage list
video:video.video:description: 50 urban artists from 17 countries converge on one of Europe's most important industrial landmarks between May and November for a show that takes advantage of the former ironworks' sprawling spaces and aura of abandonment.

video:video.video:title: Spanish authorities prepare to receive hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
video:video.video:description: An isolated staging area was being built at Tenerife's Granadilla Port ahead of the arrival of the more than 140 passengers and crew members on board the ship.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for three-day ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for three-day ceasefire

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 8th: UAE reports missile and drone attack; Trump says Iran ceasefire is holding; Man sentenced to life in prison for firebomb attack on demonstrators in Colorado; Tennessee Republicans enact new Congressional districts.

video:video.video:title: At least five people killed and 38 injured in mall fire in northwestern Mexico
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and another 38 injured on Thursday after a fire broke out at a shopping mall in the Mexican city of Los Mochis, state authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after first fatality
video:video.video:description: Health officials in several countries are trying to identify and follow people who may have been exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship. (Production by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Amazon's deforestation drop meets a tougher foe: forest degradation racing ahead
video:video.video:description: Brazil is on track to record its lowest Amazon deforestation rates since 2012. Despite this, experts warn that forest degradation, driven by wildfires, logging, and drought, continues to outpace deforestation.

video:video.video:title: Large fire spreads across Chernobyl exclusion zone as landmines hamper firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Amy Grant reflects on resisting labels people put on her
video:video.video:description: In the nearly 50 years she's been working as a musician, Amy Grant has repeatedly resisted the labels others have sought to put on her.

video:video.video:title: Star Wars' Grogu is now a movie star
video:video.video:description: Star Wars' Grogu is ready to take over the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

video:video.video:title: Are fish and chips under threat in the UK?
video:video.video:description: For years, Brockley’s Rock in southeast London has been serving customers from its Lewisham shopfront.

video:video.video:title: What did Ian McKellen find when he Googled himself?
video:video.video:description: As she promotes new drama "The Christophers," British screen icon Sir Ian McKellen reveals the furry film role he first found when he Googled himself. (May 8)

video:video.video:title: In the footsteps of a legend: a visit to the first field where Messi played
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi's football journey began at age five on the right wing, not for tactics but to keep him near his grandmother in case he cried.

video:video.video:title: IDF releases video of what it says is an operation to dismantle Gaza tunnel routes
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said Friday that its troops had “dismantled eight underground tunnel routes” spanning more than 2.5 kilometers that were used by Hamas in the southern Gaza Strip.

video:video.video:title: Kneecap sampled court case crowds on new record
video:video.video:description: How Kneecap member Mo Chara's recent court case helped to inspire a song on the Irish rappers' second album, "Fenian."

video:video.video:title: US Secretary of State comments on efforts over Iran war, relations with the Vatican
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US is expecting a response from Iran on Friday as diplomatic discussions continue.

video:video.video:title: Hungary's incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar prepares for his swearing in on Saturday
video:video.video:description: Péter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister on Saturday, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule.

video:video.video:title: AP explains the strange outbreak of hantavirus on a cruise ship
video:video.video:description: Despite a cruise ship outbreak of a rare rodent-borne illness, global health officials say the risk to the general public remains low because hantavirus germs do not easily spread between people.

video:video.video:title: Amid confrontation with the US, an Iranian museum exhibits anti-war art by American artists
video:video.video:description: While Tehran's streets had lined with anti-American billboards and posters amid the tension in the Middle East, Iranians turned out to visit an anti-war exhibition at one of the top museums.

video:video.video:title: Starmer comments after partial results show losses for Labour, wins for Reform UK in local elections
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer insisted he would carry on as prime minister despite his Labour party suffering disastrous local election losses.

video:video.video:title: Three Australian women back from Syria face slavery and terrorism charges for alleged IS group links
video:video.video:description: Three Australian women were charged on Friday with slavery and terrorism offenses after they arrived home from Syria with another 10 others whom police allege are linked to the Islamic State group.

video:video.video:title: Evidence points to human remains at home linked to Kristin Smart’s killing, sheriff says
video:video.video:description: Authorities in California say evidence suggests human remains were present at a home connected to the man convicted of killing Kristin Smart, a 19-year-old college student who went missing in 1996.

video:video.video:title: What’s in the Pentagon’s newly released UFO files?
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon has begun releasing new UFO files under President Donald Trump’s push for “maximum transparency,” including videos, photos and reports of unexplained sightings from around the world.

video:video.video:title: First batch of UFO files is released as Trump urges the public to draw its own conclusions
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon has begun releasing new files on UFOs, saying members of the public can draw their own conclusions on “unidentified anomalous phenomena."

video:video.video:title: Access to abortion pills under fire
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court this week restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a lower-court ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: AP explains upcoming Supreme Court battle over telehealth abortion pill access
video:video.video:description: A federal appeals court last week imposed new restrictions on access to the abortion pill mifepristone when it ruled against mail-order deliveries nationally.

video:video.video:title: Specialised tour at Berlin Zoo brings joy to people living with dementia and their carers
video:video.video:description: A zoo in Berlin is giving specialised tours for people living with dementia, which is bringing them and their carers joy.

video:video.video:title: Large fire spreads across Chernobyl exclusion zone as landmines hamper firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Oakland high school that produced black MLB greats invests in new players
video:video.video:description: The Oakland high school that produced Black MLB greats Frank Robinson, Curt Flood and Vada Pinson is determined to keep baseball alive — and its new head coach Jamey Smart is ready to help.

video:video.video:title: USPS considers allowing people to ship handguns through the mail
video:video.video:description: Handguns could soon be mailed through the United States Postal Service for the first time in nearly 100 years if a proposed rule under the Trump administration takes effect.

video:video.video:title: Handwriting on newly released note matches one found after Jeffrey Epstein's his death, expert says
video:video.video:description: A note that a former cellmate said he discovered after Jeffrey Epstein's first suspected jail suicide attempt was penned by the same person as a note that authorities found in the millionaire sex offender's cell after he killed himself three weeks later, handwriting expert says.

video:video.video:title: Port Authority in New York tests drone deliveries between Manhattan and Brooklyn
video:video.video:description: The trial targets a year-end goal of delivering medical supplies, and eventually expanding to other routes. (AP video by Emily Wang Fujiyama)
video:video.video:tag: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

video:video.video:title: US health officials detail plans for American passengers on hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: The U.S. agreed to send a plane to repatriate and quarantine about 17 Americans who are still on the ship who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

video:video.video:title: Palestine Marathon returns to Gaza, welcoming women and disabled athletes
video:video.video:description: On a beachside road near Nuseirat in central Gaza, which is roughly the length of a marathon, two thousand people ran a 5K race, while 15 disabled people, including amputees, completed a 2K on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Putin attends Victory Day parade in Moscow's Red Square
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II.

video:video.video:title: Janet Jackson makes rare appearance at Grammy Hall of Fame Gala
video:video.video:description: Janet Jackson made a rare appearance at the 2026 Grammy Hall of Fame Gala, where her landmark 1989 album "Rhythm Nation 1814" was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Epidemiologist in Argentina's southernmost town rejects 'smear campaign' over hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: Argentina’s tourism-dependent province — Tierra del Fuego, home to the southernmost city of Ushuaia — on Friday rejected the idea that the deadly hantavirus outbreak aboard an Atlantic cruise ship may have emerged from its territory.

video:video.video:title: Jimmy Lai's son hopes Trump's meeting with Xi could lead to breakthrough to free his father
video:video.video:description: Ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's anticipated trip to Beijing next week, the family of imprisoned media mogul and Hong Kong's pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai was hopeful of his release, Sebastien Lai, son of Jimmy Lai, said in an interview with The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee lawmakers pass new redistricting bill as state Democrats protest the vote
video:video.video:description: Tennessee enacted a new U.S. House map that carves up a majority-Black district in Memphis. Tennessee is the first state to adopt new districts since a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Memphis communities split by redistricting
video:video.video:description: After Tennessee lawmakers voted to separate Memphis into three congressional districts, Memphians fear they'll no longer have a representatives who understand and care about their communities.

video:video.video:title: Surfers in war-torn Gaza find rare moments of joy taking to the waves
video:video.video:description: Despite the severe shortages and devastation in Gaza, a handful of surfers in Gaza City continue to hold onto the sport they love.

video:video.video:title: Preparations underway at Granadilla port in Tenerife for arrival of hantavirus-stricken ship
video:video.video:description: Spanish authorities were on Saturday preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon releases video after US fires on and disables 2 more Iranian tankers
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military says its forces fired on and disabled two Iranian oil tankers Friday after exchanging fire with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz overnight.

video:video.video:title: Indonesian woman killed after Mount Dukono eruption as rescuers search for 2 missing climbers
video:video.video:description: Two Singaporean climbers remain missing as rescue operations continue amid high volcanic activity after Mount Dukono erupted on the remote island of Halmahera.

video:video.video:title: Banksy's 'The Migrant Child' unveiled after restoration, tours Venice canals
video:video.video:description: “The Migrant Child” by street artist Banksy was unveiled Thursday after restoration work and is now touring Venice’s canals for public display.

video:video.video:title: Israel military destroys residential buildings in Gaza City after ordering residents to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military destroyed residential buildings in Gaza Strip late Friday, after ordering residents to leave.

video:video.video:title: Mexico’s 3,400-year-old ulama ballgame endures against the odds
video:video.video:description: Mexico is embracing its ancient ballgame, ulama, as it prepares to co-host the 2026 FIFA World Cup. This 3,400-year-old sport, nearly erased during the Spanish conquest, is gaining attention alongside international soccer.

video:video.video:title: On day of Magyar swearing in, EU flag flies on Hungary parliament building for first time since 2014
video:video.video:description: The flag of the European Union was raised on the Hungary parliament building’s facade on Saturday afternoon for the first time since Viktor Orbán’s government removed it in 2014.

video:video.video:title: Hungary’s Péter Magyar sworn in as prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule
video:video.video:description: Hungary's Péter Magyar took his oath of office on Saturday to become the country's new prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's 16 years of autocratic rule.

video:video.video:title: Residents near Tenerife port feel divided ahead of arrival of hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: Residents in Tenerife’s southern town of San Isidro voiced a mix of fear and sympathy Saturday as a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak prepared to arrive in the Canary Islands with more than 140 passengers and crew on board.

video:video.video:title: Magyar hails 'wonderful day' in address to crowd after inauguration as Hungary PM
video:video.video:description: Hungary's Péter Magyar addressed the crowd gathered in front of the parliament building where the country’s new national assembly was inaugurated, ending Viktor Orbán’s autocratic 16-year rule.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of fatal Israeli strikes south of Beirut and in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Three Israeli drone strikes on vehicles just south of Beirut on Saturday killed four people, while a series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 13, including a man and his 12-year-old daughter, state media and the Health Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: 'Ghost of the forest' returns to Kenya as conservationists reintroduce rare antelope into the wild
video:video.video:description: The mountain bongo is being reintroduced into the wild by conservationists to increase the number of the rare antelope that are indigenous to Kenya’s forests.

video:video.video:title: Foxhounds kick off horse races at the Iroqouis Steeplechase with howling fun dog parade
video:video.video:description: At the Iroquois Steeplechase, the horses are competing for the top purses, but the fan favorite is the parade of the hounds.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 9
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Saturday, May 9th: Frontier Airlines plane hits and kills pedestrian at Denver airport; Reports of fatalities in Lebanon after Israeli strikes; Hungary’s Péter Magyar sworn in as prime minister; 3,400-year-old ballgame survives in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Mourners attend a funeral in Louisiana to remember the eight children killed in a mass shooting
video:video.video:description: A funeral was held in Louisiana for eight children who were killed in a mass shooting last month. The victims of the April 19 shooting included seven siblings who were shot by their father in an attack that also killed their cousin.

video:video.video:title: Passengers evacuate after Frontier Airlines plane hits and kills pedestrian at Denver airport
video:video.video:description: Footage obtained by The Associated Press on Saturday showed passengers evacuating an aircraft by inflatable emergency slides following an accident at Denver International Airport.

video:video.video:title: 'We've just hit somebody': Pilot tells ATC as plane hits person on Denver airport runway
video:video.video:description: The accident sparked an engine fire and forced passengers to evacuate. “We're stopping on the runway,” the pilot could be heard telling air traffic controllers, according to the site ATC.com.

video:video.video:title: 'This is not another COVID': WHO chief seeks to reassure after arrival in Tenerife
video:video.video:description: As a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship is set to arrive at Granadilla port in Tenerife, Spain on Sunday morning, the WHO, Spanish authorities and cruise company Oceanwide Expedition are coordinating the disembarkation of passengers and some crew on ground.

video:video.video:title: Dawn light shows hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius after arrival at Tenerife port
video:video.video:description: Spanish authorities were on Sunday waiting for the evacuation of more than 140 passengers and crew from the MV Hondius at Tenerife's Granadilla Port.

video:video.video:title: Meet Tornado and Twister, the two kittens rescued after tornado in Mississippi
video:video.video:description: Two aptly-named kittens, Tornado and Twister, who were rescued from the rubble of a tornado-flattened Mississippi trailer park, now have a new family.
